title: Prism3 BrandInput
description: The white-label brand-input contract: the small, validated input set the engine expands into a complete token layer. This schema is the canonical, faithful description of the engine's `BrandInput` type (engine/theme.ts) — it is validated against on every emit. The New Balance REGRESSION uses a different, richer measurement fixture (schema/nb-measured.json), not this contract.

id.description: Brand identifier; becomes the emit root/namespace seed (e.g. 'aurora' → prism.*).

primary.description: The exact brand anchor (palette 'primary'). Pinned, never shifted; the engine auto-places it on the step ramp by its lightness.

hue.description: Neutral ramp hue (deg). A small chroma tints the greys toward the brand for cohesion.

chroma.description: Peak neutral chroma (~0.004–0.02); tapers to near-0 at the ramp ends.

neutral.description: The neutral ramp generator (hue + peak chroma).

brandColors.description: OPTIONAL. Additional brand colours (secondary / tertiary / accents) — any number. Each becomes its own ramp and can be named by `actionPalette`. This is what makes the palette set open-ended.

actionPalette.description: OPTIONAL. Which palette drives the interactive/action colour. Defaults to 'primary'. Brands whose hero colour is unsuitable for actions name another palette here (an accent, or even neutral). The engine FLAGS this in notes so it is an explicit, confirmable choice.

surfaces.description: OPTIONAL. Non-default primary surfaces per mode (e.g. a warm off-white page). The contrast FLOOR moves with the declared base; the engine flags it in notes. Omit for white/black defaults.

status.description: OPTIONAL. Measured status hue overrides; omit any to let the engine synthesise from canonical hues (and carve a dedicated danger red when the primary isn't red).

disabledStrategy.description: OPTIONAL. How disabled states are coloured. 'accessible' (default): disabled text/icon/border clears `disabledMin` on the contrast floor, staying legible (the practice's contrast-preserving stance; field-rare — 0/12 surveyed systems guarantee it). 'conventional': intentionally sub-AA, leaning on the WCAG 1.4.3/1.4.11 inactive-component exemption (the field-standard dimmed look).

disabledMin.description: OPTIONAL. Contrast floor for the 'accessible' disabled strategy (default 3:1 — visibly dimmed but legible, where Primer/USWDS sit; escalates to 4.5:1 in high-contrast modes). Ignored when disabledStrategy is 'conventional'.

iconContrast.description: OPTIONAL. Icon contrast floor. 'text' (default): icons mirror text at 4.5:1. '3:1': icons resolve against the WCAG SC 1.4.11 non-text floor (graphical objects), letting secondary/semantic icons run lighter than text; icon.primary stays strong.

motionPersonality.description: OPTIONAL — the disciplined 6th input. Brand motion character. `tempo` scales the generated duration ramp; reduce-motion variants are always derived (informational preserved, vestibular → 0).

tempo.description: Scales the base duration ramp (snappy ×0.8, standard ×1.0, relaxed ×1.3).

easingEmphasized.description: Optional cubic-bezier override for the expressive 'emphasized' easing curve.

spaceBase.description: OPTIONAL. Spacing rhythm (px) for the numbered-multiplier space scale. Default 8.

radiusScale.description: OPTIONAL. Corner softness: 0 = sharp, 1 = default, 2 = soft. Default 1.

baseMd.description: OPTIONAL. The radius.md anchor (px) at scale 1. Default 4.

chroma.description: Peak ramp chroma for this status hue.

base.description: The page surface for this mode.

floorStep.description: The neutral step used as the contrast floor; defaults follow `base`.
